items.myth: If a function is continuous, it must be differentiable.
items.reality: Continuity does not guarantee a unique tangent. |x| is continuous everywhere but not differentiable at 0.
items.example: ReLU max(0,x) is continuous but has no classical derivative at x=0; optimizers use subgradient 0 or 1.

items.myth: The derivative at a maximum must always be positive before and negative after.
items.reality: At a flat maximum (plateau), f′ can be zero over an interval; higher-order information or curvature is needed.
items.example: f(x)=-x⁴ has f′(0)=0 and f″(0)=0 but is a local maximum at 0.

items.myth: Numerical gradients from autograd are always exact.
items.reality: Autograd is exact for exact operations in principle, but floating point, non-smooth ops and mixed precision introduce numerical error.
items.example: Compare `torch.autograd.gradcheck` on a custom layer—may fail if the implementation is not numerically stable.
